body_txt: The concept of Technology Levels and social traits is something that is ingrained into human society, and while forever fascinated with similar behavior in other animals there is no other sapient species to compare to. The default assumption here is Humans Are Special and we just have other human societies to compare to, so we have broad concepts of things like metallurgy, steam engines and particle physics to line up neatly in certain tiers of advancement. But perhaps in making contact with aliens you discover they somehow became a comparable civilization without a vital piece of technology, such as a nuke made of bamboo . It can provide a very alien trait to be in Powered Armor but confused by a cell phone . In Speculative Fiction an Alternate Universe can apply this to humans themselves. It's a method of Humans Through Alien Eyes . This means in a Planet of Hats situation one alien may not even know what a hat is. Despite being Rubber-Forehead Aliens , Human Aliens from a very Earth-like planet on the same Technology Level with abundant Inexplicable Cultural Ties , they are different in that they have failed to develop one very specific cultural practice. More broadly, this one change may be used to explore massive differences due to biology, environment and which iconic invention came first. Mountainous terrain may mean ziplines were more advanced than ground transportation. A lack of any illnesses means culturally they have No Sense of Personal Space . This trope can be used to pull Aesops in demonstrating how humanity wouldn't be human without love, music, sports etc. or, on the other hand, how humanity could do well without war, money, soap operas or what have you. In keeping with that, there are two ways of playing this trope: either the aliens are stumped by an Earth invention or the humans are surprised to find out that the the aliens don't have it. Sometimes the author might just be complaining about stuff he doesn't like or finds frivolous . Other times, it's just a throw-away gag. And finally, just to be clear, this trope doesn't automatically make the aliens weak . The lack of human emotions, specially this thing called love , may be used to define the alien race as well. If the aliens devote their culture to knowledge, they will be a race of Straw Vulcans . If they choose warfare instead, they will be a Proud Warrior Race or even Always Chaotic Evil ; and likely the villains. You can get the same vibe from this trope as from In Spite of a Nail , but it deals with parallel planets rather than parallel universes . Contrast Schizo Tech and Insufficiently Advanced Alien . Some members of the audience find it breaks their Willing Suspension of Disbelief , while others feel that discovering a fundamental if specific difference in an otherwise less than exotic alien culture makes it more believable. Might be the reason some Aliens Steal Cable and Alien Arts Are Appreciated . May also be the reason that there is No Such Thing as Alien Pop Culture and why Automobiles Are Alien . Contrast with the political version: Aliens Never Invented Democracy . Compare Fantasy Counterpart Appliance .
